title: Exceptions, Diagnostics, Exit Status, and Recovery · Program Brief: Exit Status Map
summary: The command catches Exception around the whole program, prints a traceback to stdout, exits zero after failure, hides the path and cause, and leaves a partial report behind. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the exit status map increment in causal error and recovery contract. Learners progress through predicti…
objectives: Map success, usage error, input error, analysis error, and output error to documented stable statuses.
Write concise actionable diagnostics containing operation and safe context while retaining a debuggable causal chain.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-exit-status-map — Map success, usage error, input error, analysis error, and output error to documented stable statuses.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-errors-exit-status-4
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-errors-exit-status-4.
# Case 1655: an offline public-library reading report.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content.
def worked_bookbot_errors_exit_status_4(expected_failures: int = 2, mapped_failures: int = 2, exit_status: int = 2, partial_output: bool = False, recovery_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "policy4dekrow",
        "revision1655",
        "commandworked41655",
        "report4dekrow1655",
        "test1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if expected_failures < 0 or mapped_failures < 0:
        return False, mapped_failures, "failure-count"
    if mapped_failures != expected_failures:
        return False, mapped_failures, "unmapped-error"
    if exit_status == 0:
        return False, exit_status, "false-success"
    if partial_output or not recovery_named:
        return False, exit_status, "unsafe-recovery"
    assert exit_status > 0
    return True, exit_status, "causal-failure"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Map success, usage error, input error, analysis error, and output error to documented stable statuses.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Exit Status Map model · Theory Exit Status Map, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Exit Status Map precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Exit Status Map model · Theory Exit Status Map, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 1150 during a multilingual community archive intake. Build deterministic Python evidence to map expected failures to causal diagnostics, stable nonzero status, atomic output, and recovery guidance; preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion; then change LF to CRLF, remove the final newline, and preserve the declared word and line policy. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Map success, usage error, input error, analysis error, and output error to documented stable statuses.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Exit Status Map model · Theory Exit Status Map, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Any nonzero exit code communicates the same recovery action.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: bookbot-exit-status-map-1rgye7.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: bookbot-exit-status-map-1rgye7(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_bookbot_exit_status_map_1rgye7\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?mapped_failures != expected_failures)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?exit_status == 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?partial_output)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not recovery_named)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
